description: Create src/interfaces/IExecutor.sol, a minimal interface that Router.sol will call into and the refactored ExecutionProxy.sol will implement. Defines a single entry point executePath(bytes32[] commands, bytes[] state) matching Weiroll VM semantics. Owning the interface as a standalone artifact lets INF-0002 (ExecutionProxy refactor) and INF-0004 (Router scaffold) land in parallel against a frozen contract.
commit: feat(interfaces): add IExecutor interface for Router<->executor boundary
steps: Create directory src/interfaces/ (does not exist yet).

Write src/interfaces/IExecutor.sol with SPDX BUSL-1.1, pragma solidity ^0.8.24, and a single interface IExecutor exposing `function executePath(bytes32[] calldata commands, bytes[] calldata state) external payable;` (payable so Router can forward native ETH to the executor).

Add a NatSpec block above the interface describing: payable so native ETH may be forwarded; no return value (Router measures outputs via balance deltas); reverts propagate to Router so atomic failure is preserved.
Run `forge fmt` on the new file.
Run `forge build` to verify the interface compiles.
acceptance_criteria: [auto] grep -n 'function executePath' src/interfaces/IExecutor.sol matches exactly one line containing `(bytes32[] calldata commands, bytes[] calldata state) external payable`.
[auto] `forge build` compiles with zero errors and zero warnings introduced by this file.
[manual] NatSpec documents the payable semantics and that Router relies on balance-diff accounting (no return value).
implementation_notes: Added src/interfaces/IExecutor.sol with the single `executePath(bytes32[] calldata commands, bytes[] calldata state) external payable` entry point and NatSpec documenting payable forwarding of native ETH, balance-diff accounting at the Router (no return value), and revert propagation preserving atomic failure. forge fmt --check and forge build both clean; no new warnings introduced by this file. Commit: d2f7e63.
